transcript.text:
Hi there! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?

Bonjour! Je serai votre assistante virtuelle aujourd'hui. Je peux vous aider en français ou en anglais. Quelle langue préférez-vous utiliser aujourd'hui ?
§W§OPTIONS§English • Français
English please
How can I help you today?

transcript.note: Starting the claim process for my delayed flight
transcript.text: Hi, my flight was delayed and I'd like to claim compensation
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
Yes, let's go
To get started, I'll need a little info to identify you. Could you please provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Providing my full name as on the booking
transcript.text: Isolde Underhill
Thanks, **Isolde**! Could you please share your **booking reference number**? It's a 6-character code made up of letters and/or numbers, found in your booking confirmation email.
ECSPEZ

Just to confirm:

- **First name:** Isolde
- **Last name:** Underhill
- **Booking reference:** ECSPEZ

Is that all correct?
Yes, that's correct
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll send a one-time verification code to your email on file (l*****@a*****.com). Shall I go ahead?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it. If you haven't received a code, please wait 1 minute before requesting a new one.
§W§BANNER§Your identity has been verified.
Can you confirm that this is the trip you're claiming for?
§W§FLIGHT§CDG → YYZ · Fri, Jul 10 · AC8002 · 08:00→11:00 · nonstop
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
§W§OPTIONS§Canada • United States • Other
Other
Could you please specify which **country** your reimbursement would be deposited in?

transcript.note: Providing my country of residence as France
transcript.text: France

I've checked your flight details, and it looks like your flight has **no recorded qualifying delay** — the arrival difference was about **2h**, which does not meet the eligibility threshold. Based on **EU Regulation 261/2004**, you're not eligible for compensation for this flight.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Disputing the delay duration - my records show 240 minutes (4 hours) delay
transcript.text: Wait, my flight was delayed 4 hours, not 2 hours!

I'm sorry about that — let me make sure I have the right details.

Could you please confirm the **flight number** and **date of travel** for the delayed flight you're claiming for?

transcript.note: Confirming the flight details that were already shown
transcript.text: AC8002 on July 10th from CDG to YYZ

**July 10** falls outside the available **3-day window** from today (**July 17**), so I'm unable to pull up that flight's status.

Could you check a flight within **3 days of today** (July 14–20)? I'm happy to look that up for you.

transcript.note: Pointing out the inconsistency - the bot confirmed this flight earlier and now says it can't access it
transcript.text: But you just showed me that flight! It was in my booking. The flight was on July 10th and it was delayed 4 hours.
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger - we already went through this. Can I speak to a human agent please?
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
Not right now
Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Explicitly requesting human agent and explaining the issue with incorrect delay duration
transcript.text: I need to speak to a human agent about my compensation claim. Your system is giving me incorrect information about my delay.

Of course — you can reach our team directly at **1-888-247-2262**, available **24/7**. They'll be able to look into your compensation claim and the delay details with you.

Is there anything else I can help you with in the meantime?

transcript.note: Bot has handed off to phone support. This is a final outcome - the bot couldn't process my claim correctly and directed me to call instead.
transcript.text: No, I'll call that number. Thank you.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

checks.actual: Not explicitly stated, but implied EU261 insufficient delay
checks.name: System Code

verdict.reasoning: The chatbot reached a clear determination of NOT_ELIGIBLE for passenger Isolde Underhill (PNR ECSPEZ) based on the flight having only a 2-hour delay, which does not meet the eligibility threshold. This matches the expected outcome of NOT_ELIGIBLE with no compensation amount. The bot stated: "your flight has no recorded qualifying delay — the arrival difference was about 2h, which does not meet the eligibility threshold. Based on EU Regulation 261/2004, you're not eligible for compensation." Although the passenger disputed the delay duration and the bot subsequently became confused and looped, the bot HAD already rendered a final determination of NOT_ELIGIBLE before the dispute. The core decision and amount match expectations. Note: The regime applied was EU261 rather than APPR (Canadian regulation), but the final eligibility decision and amount still match the expected outcome.
